title: manager-prompt-placeholder-leak
description:
A new factory-internal prompt assembly issue is visible in this window: this is separate from the existing story 26 retry concern. A flagged watcher note reports a high-severity placeholder leak in a manager_diagnostician prompt during the run ending at 2026-05-29T18:43:47.113847+00:00. Because the detector is defined to fire only when non-empty placeholder markers are present in prompt logs, this is direct evidence of template/plumbing leakage rather than normal model behavior.

The same window also contains one additional dev failure for story 26 with the unchanged ambiguous error text "tests not green after run," which continues the already-known retry pattern but does not sharpen its diagnosis. Other operational signals in scope were quiet or inconclusive: no tick events, no queue snapshots, and no spend spike evidence in-window. The prompt leak is the clearest new signal because it is attributable to factory infrastructure and is explicitly called out by detector logic.

Immediate impact in this window appears limited to a single observed manager_diagnostician run, with no direct evidence here of downstream failure caused by the leaked placeholders. Even so, the signal is non-trivial because leaked placeholders can corrupt manager-facing prompts and degrade judgment quality invisibly. A plausible explanation is a dispatch or prompt-template interpolation path leaving markers unsubstituted; the current evidence does not identify which specific placeholder or whether other personas are affected.

This concern is new rather than a continuation of the prior story-26 retry concern. Story 26 remains in a continue-the-pattern state, but the coherent, diagnosable issue in this bundle is the prompt placeholder leak in manager_diagnostician prompt assembly.

evidence.summary_excerpt: A high-severity prompt placeholder leak was also recorded in a manager_diagnostician prompt, indicating prompt assembly included placeholder text.

evidence.excerpt: manager_diagnostician run succeeded in 11.865s, cost_usd=0.080255, tokens_in=27134, tokens_out=828; this is the run the watcher note associates with the placeholder leak.
{"noteworthy":"High-severity placeholder markers were found in a manager_diagnostician prompt, which indicates prompt-template/plumbing leakage rather than normal model behavior."}
story 26 dev run failed after 39.011s with error "tests not green after run" on attempt_n=9; continuation of prior ambiguous retry pattern, not a clearer diagnosis.
{"noteworthy":"One additional dev failure for story 26 with the same ambiguous \"tests not green after run\" error; in context this continues the already-escalated repeated failure pattern."}

escalation_reason: A clearly diagnosable prompt-placeholder leakage pattern was observed in manager_diagnostician prompt assembly in this interval at 2026-05-29T18:43:47.113847+00:00. L3 judgment is needed because the detector identifies a factory plumbing issue directly attributable to prompt/dispatch infrastructure, and waiting for repetition risks silently corrupting additional manager prompts.
